data.title: New tariffs? U.S. probes pharma, chip imports' impact on national security
data.description:
The Trump administration has begun investigating the impact of pharmaceutical and semiconductor imports on national security, likely setting the stage for tariffs on the sectors.

The Department of Commerce's pharma investigation covers generic and non-generic drugs, active pharma ingredients, and derivative products. The semiconductor probe covers items including leading-edge chips, chipmaking equipment, silicon wafers, and "downstream products that contain semiconductors."

The probes will look into current and projected demand for the pharma and semiconductor products, the ability of domestic production to meet demand, and potential for export restrictions. The chip probe will also assess "the ability of foreign nations to weaponize their control over semiconductors and SME supply chains."

The investigations have been initiated under Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962, which allows the president to adjust imports if they are deemed to be a threat to national security. 

Section 232 probes must be completed within 270 days of initiation. Public comments on the investigations will be open 21 days after Wednesday.

The investigations follow the Trump administration exempting chips, smartphones, computers and other electronics imports from the 145% tariffs for Chinese imports. Pharma and semiconductor imports are also exempt from the baseline 10% tariff that went into effect on April 5.

Pharma stocks to watch: Merck (MRK), J&J (JNJ), Eli Lilly (LLY), Novartis (NVS), Novo Nordisk (NVO), AstraZeneca (AZN), 

Semiconductor stocks to watch: Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM), ASML (ASML), Nvidia (NVDA), Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF).

data.title: Trump admin to discard rule that caps credit card late fee at $8
France inflation rate steady at 0.8% as expected
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in France was confirmed at 0.8% in March 2025, the same as in February, remaining at its lowest level since February 2021.

Steeper declines in energy prices and manufactured goods were offset by rising prices in services, particularly insurance, as well as food, especially fresh products.  Meanwhile, tobacco prices rose at a slower pace.

On a monthly basis, consumer prices increased by 0.2%, following stagnation in the previous period, in line with initial estimates. 

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWQ)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Barksdale Announces to raise up to C$3M in private placement
Beiersdorf Aktiengesellschaft reports Q1 results; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Beiersdorf Aktiengesellschaft press release (OTCPK:BDRFF): Q1 Revenue of €2.7B (+3.8% Y/Y), (+3.6% in organic terms).
  • Consumer Business Segment: Sales reach €2.3 billion (+2.3% in organic terms) against strong prior-year baseline
  • Growth for NIVEA +2.5%, Derma +11.4%, Health Care +10.8%; La Prairie –17.5%.
  • tesa Business Segment: Strong sales growth to €441 million (+10.7% in organic terms).
  • Guidance for 2025 confirmed: Organic sales growth of 4-6% expected for the Group and the Consumer Business Segment.

data.title: UK's unemployment rate steady at 4.4% in February
Recharge buys Giftcloud from Groupon
data.description:
Recharge, the European prepaid payments leader, has acquired Giftcloud from Groupon (NASDAQ:GRPN), marking its strategic expansion into the B2B digital rewards market.

Giftcloud's platform supports engagement campaigns for major UK companies (Vodafone, Sky, TalkTalk), handles millions of transactions yearly, and provides Recharge with a proven and scalable B2B offering.

The acquisition follows Recharge's recent €45M facility with ABN AMRO for M&A activities.

Giftcloud's technology will enable Recharge to directly serve business clients, and the acquisition significantly expands Recharge's UK presence, its third-largest market and a platform for future B2B growth in Europe.

This acquisition represents Recharge's second major purchase following Startselect in 2023.

data.title: Nissan to cut production of top-selling Rogue SUV in response to Trump tariffs - report
TomTom GAAP EPS of €0.02, revenue of €140.4M
data.description:
• TomTom N.V. press release (OTCPK:TMOAF): Q1 GAAP EPS of €0.02.
  • Revenue of €140.4M (+0.8% Y/Y).
  • Location Technology revenue increased by 2% to €121 million (Q1 '24: €119 million).
  • Automotive operational revenue increased by 5% to €83 million (Q1 '24: €79 million).
  • Free cash flow was an outflow of €3 million (Q1 '24: outflow of €9 million).
  • Net cash of €257 million (Q4 '24: €264 million).

data.title: Publicis Groupe reports Q1 results; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Publicis Groupe S.A. press release (OTCQX:PUBGY): Q1 Revenue of €3.54B (+9.6% Y/Y), including organic growth of +4.9%.
  • The Groupe’s liquidity remains very solid at €4.2 billion.
  • Reaffirming full year 2025 guidance: Organic growth of +4 to +5% o Slight improvement in margin vs. industry-high level of 18% in 2024 o €1.9 to €2.0 billion free cash flow generation.
